ddls:
CREATE TABLE "contact_lenses" (
  "lens_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contact lens */,
  "lens_name" TEXT /* Name of the contact lens product */,
  "power_range" TEXT /* Power range (e.g., +1.00 to -9.00) */,
  "base_curve" REAL /* Base curve of the lens */,
  "diameter" REAL /* Diameter of the lens */,
  "material" TEXT /* Material of the lens */,
  "water_content" TEXT /* Water content percentage */,
  "wearing_type" TEXT /* Type of wearing (e.g., daily, monthly) */,
  "price" REAL /* Price per box of lenses */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color options for colored lenses */,
  "brand" TEXT /* Brand of the contact lens */,
  "package_size" INTEGER /* Number of lenses in a package */,
  "uv_protection" INTEGER /* Indicates if the lens has UV protection */,
  "is_toric" INTEGER /* Indicates if the lens is designed for astigmatism */,
  "is_multifocal" INTEGER /* Indicates if the lens is designed for multifocal vision */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the supplier of the lens */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("lens_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contact_lenses_supplier_id FOREIGN KEY ("supplier_id") REFERENCES suppliers ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory record */,
  "lens_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the contact lens product */,
  "stock_quantity" INTEGER /* Current stock quantity */,
  "reorder_level" INTEGER /* Minimum quantity before reorder is triggered */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the inventory was last updated */,
  "reorder_quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity to reorder when stock falls below the reorder level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_lens_id FOREIGN KEY ("lens_id") REFERENCES contact_lenses ("lens_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "suppliers" (
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supplier */,
  "supplier_name" TEXT /* Name of the supplier */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the supplier */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the supplier */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the supplier */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the supplier */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the contact person at the supplier */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_items" (
  "order_item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order item */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the order containing this item */,
  "lens_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the contact lens product */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the lens ordered */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the lens at the time of order */,
  "promotion_id" INTEGER /* Reference to any active promotion applied to the lens */,
  "discount_amount" REAL /* Amount of discount applied to the lens */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_promotion_id FOREIGN KEY ("promotion_id") REFERENCES promotions ("promotion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_lens_id FOREIGN KEY ("lens_id") REFERENCES contact_lenses ("lens_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "account_creation_date" TEXT /* Date the customer account was created */,
  "last_purchase_date" TEXT /* Date of the last purchase */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales" (
  "sale_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sale */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the order associated with this sale */,
  "sale_date" TEXT /* Date the sale occurred */,
  "total_amount" REAL /* Total amount of the sale */,
  "order_source" TEXT /* Source of the order (e.g., online, in-store) */,
  "sales_representative_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sales representative who processed the sale */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sale_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "returns" (
  "return_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each return record */,
  "order_item_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the order item being returned */,
  "return_date" TEXT /* Date the return was processed */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of lenses being returned */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the return */,
  "refund_amount" REAL /* Amount refunded for the return */,
  "return_method" TEXT /* Method of return (e.g., in-store, mail) */,
  "return_status" TEXT /* Current status of the return (e.g., pending, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("return_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_returns_order_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_item_id") REFERENCES order_items ("order_item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "promotions" (
  "promotion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each promotion */,
  "lens_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the contact lens product */,
  "promotion_description" TEXT /* Description of the promotion */,
  "discount_percentage" REAL /* Discount percentage offered */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the promotion */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the promotion */,
  "promotion_type" TEXT /* Type of promotion (e.g., percentage, fixed amount) */,
  "max_discount" REAL /* Maximum discount allowed */,
  "minimum_spend" REAL /* Minimum spend required to qualify for the promotion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("promotion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotions_lens_id FOREIGN KEY ("lens_id") REFERENCES contact_lenses ("lens_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer providing feedback */,
  "lens_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the contact lens product being reviewed */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the customer */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments from the customer */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date the feedback was given */,
  "is_public" INTEGER /* Indicates if the feedback is public or private */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_lens_id FOREIGN KEY ("lens_id") REFERENCES contact_lenses ("lens_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
